zh.lifeSummary: 當William Baker於1482年出生在英國英格蘭素福爾可時，他的父親Joseph Jackson Baker22歲，母親Ann Pickering13歲。他於1517年與Margery Hawis在英國英格蘭結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於1558年12月23日在英國素福爾可赫普沃斯享年76歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当William Baker于1482年出生在英国英格兰素福爾可时，他的父亲Joseph Jackson Baker22岁，母亲Ann Pickering13岁。他于1517年与Margery Hawis在英国英格兰结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和1个女儿的父母。他去世于1558年12月23日在英国素福爾可赫普沃斯享年76岁。

nameMeanings.name: Baker
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: occupational name, from Middle English bakere, Old English bæcere, a derivative of bacan ‘to bake’. It may have been used for someone whose special task in the kitchen of a great house or castle was the baking of bread, but since most humbler households did their own baking in the Middle Ages, it may also have referred to the owner of a communal oven used by the whole village. The right to be in charge of this and exact money or loaves in return for its use was in many parts of the country a hereditary feudal privilege. Compare Miller . Less often the surname may have been acquired by someone noted for baking particularly fine bread or by a baker of pottery or bricks.

Americanized form (translation into English) of surnames meaning ‘baker’, for example Dutch Bakker , German Becker and Beck , French Boulanger and Bélanger (see Belanger ), Czech Pekař, Slovak Pekár, and Croatian Pekar .

History: Baker was established as an early immigrant surname in Puritan New England. Among others, two men called Remember Baker (father and son) lived at Woodbury, CT, in the early 17th century, and an Alexander Baker arrived in Boston, MA, in 1635.

variants.name: Miller
meanings: English and Scottish: occupational name for a miller. The standard modern vocabulary word represents the northern Middle English term miller, an agent derivative of mille ‘mill’, reinforced by Old Norse mylnari (see Milner ). In southern, western, and central England Millward (literally, ‘mill keeper’) was the usual term. In North America, the surname Miller has absorbed many cognate surnames from other languages, for example German Müller (see Mueller ), Dutch Mulder and Molenaar , French Meunier , Italian Molinaro , Spanish Molinero , Hungarian Molnár (see Molnar ), Slovenian, Croatian, and Serbian Mlinar , Polish Młynarz or Młynarczyk (see Mlynarczyk ). Miller (including in the senses below) is the seventh most frequent surname in the US.

South German, Swiss German, and Jewish (Ashkenazic): variant of Müller ‘miller’ (see Mueller ) and, in North America, also an altered form of this. This form of the surname is also found in other European countries, notably in Poland, Denmark, France (mainly Alsace and Lorraine), and Czechia; compare 3 below.
Americanized form of Polish, Czech, Croatian, Serbian, and Slovenian   Miler   ‘miller’, a surname of German origin.

variants.name: Baxter
meanings: English: occupational name from Middle English baxter ‘baker’ (from Old English bæcestre ‘baker’, earlier ‘female baker’, the feminine equivalent of bæcere). Compare Baker .

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

en.lifeSummary: When William Baker was born in 1482, in Suffolk, England, United Kingdom, his father, Joseph Jackson Baker, was 22 and his mother, Ann Pickering, was 13. He married Margery Hawis in 1517, in England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 1 daughter. He died on 23 December 1558, in Hepworth, Suffolk, England, at the age of 76.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque William Baker est né en 1482, à Suffolk, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Joseph Jackson Baker, avait 22 ans et sa mère, Ann Pickering, avait 13 ans. Il a épousé Margery Hawis en 1517, à Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 1 fille. Il est décédé le 23 décembre 1558, à Hepworth, Suffolk, Angleterre, à l’âge de 76 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als William Baker 1482 in Suffolk, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war sein Vater Joseph Jackson Baker 22 und seine Mutter Ann Pickering 13. Er hat 1517 in England, United Kingdom Margery Hawis geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Er starb am 23. Dezember 1558 im Alter von 76 Jahren in Hepworth, Suffolk, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando William Baker è nato il nel 1482 nel Suffolk, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, il suo padre, Joseph Jackson Baker, aveva 22 anni e il suo madre, Ann Pickering, aveva 13 anni. Lui sposato Margery Hawis nel 1517, nel Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 1 figlia. Morì il 23 dicembre 1558, nel Hepworth, Suffolk, England, all'età di 76 anni.

ko.lifeSummary: William Baker(이)가 1482년 영국 잉글랜드 수폴크에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Joseph Jackson Baker(은)는 22세였고, 그의 어머니 Ann Pickering(은)는 13세였다. 그는 1517년 영국 잉글랜드에서 Margery Hawis(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1558년 12월 23일 영국 수폴크 헤p워스에서 76세의 나이로 사망했다.
